examples.input: Summarize the findings of quantum dot LED breakthroughs in 2025.
examples.output:
# Executive Summary
Quantum dot LEDs (QLEDs) achieved a breakthrough in external quantum efficiency (EQE) using a new cadmium-free colloidal formulation, raising efficiency levels from 18% to 24.5% [1]. This development marks a significant milestone toward heavy-metal-free consumer displays.

# Methodology
Researchers utilized high-resolution transient absorption spectroscopy to track charge carrier dynamics [2]. Synthesized ligand-passivated ZnSe/ZnS core-shell architectures were deployed in a standard device structure and evaluated over 10,000 continuous hours [3].

# Findings
- Cadmium-free emissions achieved exceptional purity with a full-width at half-maximum (FWHM) of under 20 nm [1].
- Lifetime performance (T50) increased by 40% under high-density current conditions [3].

examples.input: Detail the methodology of the recent deep-sea hydrothermal vent temperature survey.
examples.output:
# Executive Summary
An oceanographic expedition mapped hydrothermal venting anomalies in the Mariana Trench, identifying temperature excursions up to 380°C in diffuse flow zones [1].

# Methodology
Deployments of the Jason-class ROV utilized deep-sea conductivity-temperature-depth (CTD) sensor chains [2]. In-situ fluid samples were collected using specialized titanium gas-tight bottles [3]. Analysis of helium-3 isotopic ratios validated thermal plumes.

# Findings
- Thermal vents are highly concentrated within a 1.2-kilometer fault line zone [1].
- Methane gas levels inside venting fluids indicate high microbiological activity [3].
